issue_title: Admin UI - unable to load user's groups when large number of groups defined for the realm
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I have a Keycloak instance with ~18k defined groups in flat structure - every group on the same root level.  
When I go to security admin console (admin UI) -> Users -> select sample user and go to "Groups" tab, only "loading" circle is displayed and no groups at all.  

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/47423695/2be865b9-ce64-445a-aed4-f99ac7b36321)

Looking at network requests - I can see two requests:  
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/47423695/9ff3ba2b-0f63-4175-968f-59aab45b132c)

1. https://mydomain/auth/admin/realms/myrealm/users/12345679/groups?first=0&max=11
2. https://mydomain/auth/admin/realms/myrealm/groups

The second requests takes "forever" to complete (~5 minutes in my case), seems to be performance problem with getting all realm groups. Anyway this request seems to be pointless because "Groups" tab displays only first 10 groups by default and results are paginated.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

User's groups in admin UI should be displayed in acceptable time, independently from groups count for whole realm.
Probably: API request to get all realm groups should not be executed on User's groups tab.

### Actual behavior

User's groups in Admin UI are not displayed, because UI calls API to get all realm's groups. Request finished after ~5 minutes when there are ~18k groups defined.

### How to Reproduce?

Fill any Keycloak instance (version 21.1.1) with 18k groups on root level (optional).
Go to security admin console -> Users -> select any user and go to "Groups" tab. Open browser's developer tools (F12) and open Network tab. Refresh page, you'll see there are two requests for user's group: one with max=11 parameter, and second request getting all realm groups.
When you have ~18k groups in the realm then no user's groups will be displayed at all because UI waits till requests for all groups finish.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Enabled User Event Types not visible when "Save events" disabled.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When disabling the "Save events" toggle, one can no longer see the list of Event types that would be emitted to any configured event listeners.

Being able to modify this list is important because despite the fact that the "Save events" toggle is on or off, any custom event listeners can still receive and act upon the emitted events that are present. In some cases a user may wish to disable some set of these, or is upgrading from prior versions of Keycloak where new event types would need to be manually enabled and would need to add those to the list.

The use case is that we do not want the events to go to the database at all, hence "Save events" would be off - however we want them to go through our custom event listener(s) and being able to see what event types can go through that listener is useful and important.

As of now, we simply toggle "on" the Save events feature to edit through the UI temporarily, or use the Admin API directly for this function.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

User Event Types that are emitted within Keycloak are editable despite the "Save events" toggle.

### Actual behavior

Event Types that are allowed/emitted within Keycloak are only editable when the save events toggle is on.

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/4944041/0acbea78-04bb-4c49-9798-a09d8bd4767b)


### How to Reproduce?

Run Keycloak
Go to Realm settings -> Events tab
Select the "User events settings" sub tab
Enable the Save events, Click Save
Disable the Save Events
Event list/table no longer presented at the bottom.


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Ensure proper escaping  for LDAP
issue_body:
From #20396
[...] I used the command line to do some interactive testing using 

```
./kcadm.sh get users -q "search=Wilson"
```

The results are ok. 

Examples:

* `Wil*son` -> finds entries with wildcard in LDAP, but not in JPA, as JPA only replaces asterisks at the beginning and the end, and `*` is a natural wildcard in LDAP.
* `Wil%son` -> finds entries with wildcard in JPA, but not LDAP, as `%` is a natural wildcard in JPA
* `Wil)son` -> returns a 400 error as the LDAP doesn't properly escape braces in this search mode

Possible changes in that follow-up PR: 
* Define which wildcards would be supported in the frontend (possibly only `*`?)
* For JPA, replace all `*` with `%`, and escape all other wildcards like `_`
* For LDAP, keep all `*`, and escape all other characters

_Originally posted by @ahus1 in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/20396#pullrequestreview-1436667356_

issue_title: [Declarative User Profile] Optional attributes become required
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When the declarative user profile is enabled, adding new attributes and leaving the required toggle _off_ causes the attributes to be required, when editing the user or the attribute itself afterwards.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Attributes created with the required toggle set to _off_ have that toggle set to off, when editing them after creation and when editing user profiles, the attributes don't have to be specified.

### Actual behavior

After creating the attribute with the required toggle set to _off_, it must be specified when editing a user and when editing the attribute itself, the setting is set to _on_.

### How to Reproduce?

1. start keycloak 21.1.1 with declarative user profiles enabled `docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin -e KC_FEATURES=declarative-user-profile quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:21.1.1 start-dev`
2. log in to the admin ui
3. create a new realm, go to realm settings and set "User Profile Enabled" to on
4. go to the "User Profile" tab in realm settings and create a new attribute with "Required" set to _off_

When editing the create attribute again, "Required" is set to _on_ now and when editing users, the attribute must be specified.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: `register-node-at-startup` in EAP Client Adapter eventually causes "java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: unable to create native thread
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues

### Area

### Describe the bug

In this file there is an ExecutorService created that is never closed:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/41b9a75f1ee3da250b402c4ec6b9cfede0ea1bac/adapters/oidc/adapter-core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/NodesRegistrationManagement.java#L39

I believe the OOM issue is visible only with the elytron adapter which does not cache `NodeRegistrationManagement` object while other adapters do (at least it seems so).
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/41b9a75f1ee3da250b402c4ec6b9cfede0ea1bac/adapters/oidc/wildfly-elytron/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/elytron/KeycloakHttpServerAuthenticationMechanism.java#L132


### Version

21.1.x

### Expected behavior

ExecutorService is closed or the object is cached

### Actual behavior

Each request create a new object of `NodesRegistrationManagement`

### How to Reproduce?

Use `register-node-at-startup` option for the adapter

### Anything else?

issue_title: Identity providers initialization has to use models
issue_body: The type of `IdentityProviderModel` instances depends on the actual identity provider which is not respected in `MapIdentityProviderEntity.toModel` method. This leads to the situation where an identity provider overrides some behaviour (e.g. `GoogleIdentityProviderConfig`) but this is not reflected in `MapRealmAdapter.getIdentityProvidersStream`.

issue_title: keycloak-admin-client does not url-encode client id and secret for basic auth as defined in RFC6749
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/client/node

### Describe the bug

According to [RFC6749 section 2.3.1](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1), client id and secret need to be URLencoded before being base64 encoded for basic auth.
This is related to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/11908.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Client ID and secret are first URL-encoded and then Base64-encoded for basic auth.

### Actual behavior

Client id and secret are only Base64-encoded for basic auth.

### How to Reproduce?

Check source code.

### Anything else?

The respective code is  https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/7502a31421d8d80483cb840e35696dfb2634ba3f/js/libs/keycloak-admin-client/src/utils/auth.ts#L82
commit_message: Ensure basic auth header is encoded properly (#20896)

We've encountered particular issues while executing tests from the `integration-arquillian/` module.

```
12:49:23,604 INFO  [org.keycloak.testsuite.arquillian.undertow.KeycloakOnUndertow] Starting auth server on embedded Undertow on: http://0.0.0.0:8180
```

issue_title: Inconsistent Wildcard handling for JPA
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

As a follow-up on #20510, we found that when searching for users, JPA allows wildcards `%` and `_` when searching, while other providers like LDAP allow only `*`. As `*` is a natural wildcard character in LDAP, it works in all places, while with JPA it only works at the beginning and the end of the search string. 



### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

The only wildcard characters should be `*` which should work consistently across all providers in all places in the search string. All special characters in a specific provider like `%` and `_` for JPA should be escaped.

### Actual behavior

An asterisk work with JPA only the beginning and end of the string, but not in the middle. Native JPA wildcard work in the search string anywhere in the string.

### How to Reproduce?

Use 

```
./kcadm.sh get users -q "search=Wilson"
```

with 

* `Wil*son` -> finds entries with wildcard in LDAP, but not in JPA, as JPA only replaces asterisks at the beginning and the end, and `*` is a natural wildcard in LDAP.
* `Wil%son` -> finds entries with wildcard in JPA, but not LDAP, as `%` is a natural wildcard in JPA


### Anything else?

LDAP was handled to support `*` as expected in #20510
commit_message:
Inconsistent Wildcard handling for JPA (#21671)

* Inconsistent Wildcard handling for JPA

Closes #20610

Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: Moving a group to root loses all its members
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the Admin UI, when a group "ChildGroup" that is a child of another group "ParentGroup" is moved to the root, all members associated with  "ChildGroup" group are lost because the group is deleted and then recreated with a new ID.

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

Any members of ChildGroup prior to the move would still be members after the move. The group ID of ChildGroup should not change.

### Actual behavior

ChildGroup has no members after the move. The group ID for ChildGroup changed to a different value.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Set up a Keycloak realm with two groups:
	ParentGroup - as a root-level group
	ChildGroup - as a child of ParentGroup
2. Add one or more members to ChildGroup
3. Either from the group hierarchy view, or on the "Child groups" tab of the ParentGroup, click the ellipsis on the row for ChildGroup, and select "Move to" from the popup menu.
4. In the move dialog, don't select a new parent (leave as "Move ChildGroup to root"), and click "Move here"
5. After ChildGroup is moved to be a root-level group, check its list of members.

### Anything else?

Research findings:
The admin UI is performing the following requests to the Keycloak API during the move operation:

`GET /admin/realms/MYREALM/groups?first=0&max=11`
Returns the set of groups, including the nested child group with an ID of 4201f8c9-73a4-4071-bbc3-20583c3d0235
`DELETE /admin/realms/MYREALM/groups/4201f8c9-73a4-4071-bbc3-20583c3d0235`
Deletes the nested child group and all of its members
`POST /admin/realms/MYREALM/groups`
Creates a new group with an ID of feb1fdb8-9cc8-494f-b496-c8389acdadfb
`GET /admin/realms/MYREALM/groups/4201f8c9-73a4-4071-bbc3-20583c3d0235/role-mappings`
Returns 404 since that group was deleted
`GET /admin/realms/MYREALM/groups/4201f8c9-73a4-4071-bbc3-20583c3d0235/members?first=0&max=11`
Returns 404 since that group was deleted
`GET /admin/realms/MYREALM/groups/4201f8c9-73a4-4071-bbc3-20583c3d0235`
Returns 404 since that group was deleted
`GET /admin/realms/MYREALM/ui-ext/groups?first=0&max=21&exact=false`
Returns the set of groups, including a root-level group with an ID of feb1fdb8-9cc8-494f-b496-c8389acdadfb, but no group with an ID of 4201f8c9-73a4-4071-bbc3-20583c3d0235
`GET /admin/realms/MYREALM/ui-ext/groups?first=0&max=21&exact=false`
Same result as the previous request

Examining the admin-ui client code in js/apps/admin-ui/src/groups/components/MoveDialog.tsx, the function "moveToRoot" begins with the following lines, which appear to be responsible for the DELETE and POST requests above:
```
  await adminClient.groups.del({ id: source.id! });
  const { id } = await adminClient.groups.create({
    ...source,
    id: undefined,
  });
```

In contrast, the "moveToGroup" function, which is used for all other group moves, performs the following operation:
`    await adminClient.groups.setOrCreateChild({ id: dest.id! }, source);`
This results in the following API request:
`POST /admin/realms/MYREALM/groups/{parent-group-id}/children`
Which makes the posted group object a child of the specified parent group. This operation works correctly without losing the members associated with the group. However, it won't work for moving to the root-level, since there is no parent group to specify.

After some research, I found a solution, which involves performing the following API request:
`POST /admin/realms/MYREALM/groups`
With a JSON object payload containing at least two properties, the "id" and "name" from the group to be moved to the root-level.

I have verified that this method works correctly for moving a group from a child position to the root-level without loss of members. It appears that this is the intended way for groups to be moved to root, since the addTopLevelGroup method in the services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/GroupsResource.java file contains code specifically for this purpose as follows:

```
        String groupName = rep.getName();

        if (ObjectUtil.isBlank(groupName)) {
            throw ErrorResponse.error("Group name is missing", Response.Status.BAD_REQUEST);
        }

        try {
            if (rep.getId() != null) {
                child = realm.getGroupById(rep.getId());
                if (child == null) {
                    throw new NotFoundException("Could not find child by id");
                }
                realm.moveGroup(child, null);
                adminEvent.operation(OperationType.UPDATE).resourcePath(session.getContext().getUri());

```

Based on this, I believe the change that would correct the move behavior is to replace the first few lines in the "moveToRoot" function referenced previously with the following code.

```
  const { id } = source;
  await adminClient.groups.create(source);

```
commit_message:
Use a different API method to move groups (#20820)

Closes #20615

Co-authored-by: daniel-brannon <daniel.brannon@ososlo.com>

issue_title: FAPI 2.0 security profile - Reject Implicit Grant executor does not return an appropriate error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

The merged [FAPI 2.0 Baseline : Reject Implicit Grant](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/8574) does not returne an appropriate error.

It returns `invalid_grant`.
However, according to [OIDC](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#AuthError) and [OAuth2](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6749#section-4.1.2.1) specs `invalid_request` or `unsupported_response_type` are appropriate.

### Version

current snapshot (kc21.1.1)

### Expected behavior

It returns `invalid_request`.

### Actual behavior

It returned `invalid_grant`.

### How to Reproduce?

Send an authorizaiton request of Implicit grant when Reject Implicit Grant executor is activated.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
FAPI 2.0 security profile - Reject Implicit Grant executor does not return an appropriate error

Closes #20622

issue_title: High severity - Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm vulnerability in org.bitbucket.b_c:jose4j
issue_body:
**Source**: Snyk Open Source
**Location**: pom.xml
This file introduces a vulnerable org.bitbucket.b_c:jose4j package with a high severity vulnerability.
org.bitbucket.b_c:jose4j@0.7.11

* Package Manager: maven
* Vulnerable module: org.bitbucket.b_c:jose4j
* Introduced through: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT, org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT and others
### Detailed paths
* _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT › org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT › org.wildfly.security:wildfly-elytron@2.1.0.Final › org.wildfly.security:wildfly-elytron-http-oidc@2.1.0.Final › org.bitbucket.b_c:jose4j@0.7.11
# Overview
[org.bitbucket.b_c:jose4j](https://bitbucket.org/b_c/jose4j) is a robust and easy to use open source implementation of JSON Web Token (JWT) and the JOSE specification suite (JWS, JWE, and JWK). It is written in Java and relies solely on the JCA APIs for cryptography. Please see https://bitbucket.org/b_c/jose4j/wiki/Home for more info, examples, etc...

issue_title: Reset password flow fails with "Page has expired" error when Kerberos authentication is enabled in the browser flow
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When Kerberos authentication is enabled in the browser flow, non-kerberos authentication user cannot complete the reset password flow because "Page has expired" error happens.

### Version

RH-SSO 7.6

### Expected behavior

HTTP 200 success response should be returned after resetting the password.

### Actual behavior

HTTP 401 error response is returned after resetting the password.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a new realm
2. Create a new RH-SSO local user
3. Make "Kerberos" execution in the "Browser" flow requirement "ALTERNATIVE"
(No need to configure kerberos User Federation config for reproducing the error)
4. Make "Forgot password" option "ON"
5. Go "Realm Settings" -> "Email" tab, and configure "Host" and "From" fields. 
6. Open the login form page
7. Navigate to the password reset form page by clicking "Forgot Password?" link
8. Fill the "Username or email" field with the user created in the step2, and submit

The request will be processed properly, the reset link email will be sent, but the HTTP response will have  HTTP status code 401 and "WWW-Authenticate: Negotiate" header, which instructs the browser to do Kerberos authentication.
If the browser is not configured to handle Kerberos authentication, the browser will re-submit the same HTTP request,
but RH-SSO server will reject the request because the session code in the request is already consumed in the previous request, and RH-SSO server will return the "Page has expired" page.

There is a similar issue([KEYCLOAK-17218](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-17218)),  but the issue still persists in RH-SSO 7.6.0.

### Anything else?

RH-SSO server must not add "WWW-Authenticate: Negotiate" header to the "Reset password" response even when Kerberos authentication execution is enabled.

**Workaround(only works for wildfly based distribution)**
Following JBoss CLI commands mitigates the issue by removing the "WWW-Authenticate: Negotiate" header from the "Reset password" response.

```
/subsystem=undertow/configuration=filter/expression-filter=clear-www-authenticate-header-filter:add(expression="clear(attribute='%{o,WWW-Authenticate}', pre-commit='true')")
/subsystem=undertow/server=default-server/host=default-host/filter-ref=clear-www-authenticate-header-filter:add(predicate="method(POST) and path-prefix(/auth/realms/<PUT_YOUR_REALM_NAME_HERE>/login-actions/reset-credentials)")
```
Please note this workaround only works for Wildfly(JBoss-EAP) based distribution. For Quarkus-based distribution, it cannot be used and need to fix the root cause.

issue_title: "Not username" and "Not email" options when creating/changing a password, should ignore letter case when comparing password to email or to username
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

When enabling the "Not username" and "not email" options for when users create/change their password, there ais a case in which certain passwords are allowed but shouldn't be.
What happens is that email and username are always saved in lower case letters, even if they were inputted in upper case letters.
As a result, if I use a password with even one upper case, it will never match either email or username.

Examples:
1. Password and email both inputted as: A@b.c will be allowed, since password will be saved as "A@b.c" and email will be saved as "a@b.c"
2. Password and username both inputted as Abcd1 will also be allowed, since password will be saved as "Abcd1" and username will be saved as "abcd1"

In my opinion, you should compare password to email and password to username, with ignore case. Meaning, the same text in 2 fields, with the only difference between them being a lower or upper case letter, should be considered as identical values.


### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

"ABC" should be considered equal to "abc", when comparing password to email or username.

### Actual behavior

Currently, "Abc" is not considered equal to "abc".

### How to Reproduce?

There are many flows that can reproduce this issue: change/reset password to match the username, or create a user with email and password identical, except that the password has upper case letters.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
'Not username' and 'Not email' options when creating/changing a password, should ignore letter case when comparing password to email or to username (#22804)

Signed-off-by: Pedro Henrique Silva <pesilva@redhat.com>

When "Revalidate Client Certificate" is active and there's no reverse proxy before Keycloak the certificate won't be revalidated. issue_title: Userinfo endpoint doesn't accept charset
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

After https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/15429 `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` header is required to be passed for token introspection endpoint:
```java
if (MediaType.APPLICATION_FORM_URLENCODED.equalsIgnoreCase(headers.getHeaderString(HttpHeaders.CONTENT_TYPE))) {
      // some code
 }
```

Thus it rejects a valid header when `charset` is specified, e.g. `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded;charset=UTF-8` which is a default value added to all form requests in some popular Java libraries. For instance, 
https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/blob/main/spring-web/src/main/java/org/springframework/http/converter/FormHttpMessageConverter.java#L420.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

`Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded;charset=UTF-8` is accepted. 

### Actual behavior

`Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` is only accepted as is.

### How to Reproduce?

```bash 
curl -u $client_auth $url/realms/$realm/protocol/openid-connect/userinfo \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded;charset=UTF-8' -d "access_token=$atoken"
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Userinfo endpoint doesn't accept charset #20671

Closes 20671

issue_title: Realm export performance heavily depends on the amount of users per file
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

import-export

### Describe the bug

I frequently need to export a realm (including users) and need to import it into another Keycloak server.

I have a realm with currently around 11500 users. Exporting the realm with users is extremely slow if --users=same_file is used. The export then exports just a few users per second and runs into a timeout after 5 minutes. After this time, only around 930 users have been exported, amounting to around 3 users per second.

If --users=different_files is used, the export is fairly fast if a low number of users per file (via the --users-per-file parameter) is used and becomes slow if a larger amount of users per file is used. With 50 users per file (the default), the whole export takes around 80 seconds (over 140 users per second), with 1000 users per file, the export takes 280 seconds (around 40 users per second), with a higher number this runs into a timeout. The amount of users per file (which I assume is also the transaction size) heavily affects the performance during the export.

The relationship between the number of users per file and the export time is not mentioned anywhere in the Keycloak documentation.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

The number of users per file during a realm export should not impact the performance. Keycloak should internally always work with some fixed optimal transaction size, regardless of how many users it stores per file in the end. It should not matter whether I use 10 or 5000 users per file (except for some slight overhead for having more files).

I expect the export time to grow linearly with the number of users.


### Actual behavior

The realm export performance depends heavily on the amount of users per file. The worst case is --users=same_file, which is very slow. With --users=different_files, the performance gets better and better the lower the --users-per-file parameter is.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a realm with a large number of users (e.g. 10,000). Measure the time for a realm export using --users=same_file and compare this to using --users=different_files with different values for --users-per-file (50, 500, 5000).

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: FAPI 2.0 security profile - not allow an authorization request whose parameters were not included in Request Object pushed to PAR request
issue_body:
### Description

According to [FAPI 2.0 security profile](https://openid.bitbucket.io/fapi/fapi-2_0-security-profile.html), all necessary request parameters need to be included  in an authorization request directly to the PAR endpoint. Therefore, if a client sends an authorization request to Authorization endpoint, but the request violates the point above, an authorization server needs to return an error response.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/20623 resolved it. However, it does not treat request object pushed to PAR endpoint.
When a request object is pushed to PAR endpoint, parsing the request object is needed.

issue_title: Denial of Service/100% CPU usage: CRLUtils in infinite loop if more than one CRL list is used from different CAs
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

X.509 login flow is used and CRL check is configured and activated.
It is working with one CRL list from one CA.
But possible client certificates are comming from two different CAs, thus two CRL lists are configured.

In CRLUtils we catch the path to `findCRLSignatureCertificateInTruststore()` and there we are in an infinite loop if more than one CRL list is used from different CAs.
We never break the `while (true) {}` loop, CPU usage is 100% and keycloak does not longer respond - a denial of service.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/utils/CRLUtils.java#L121

As a proof of failure I added some debug outputs:
```
    private static X509Certificate findCRLSignatureCertificateInTruststore(KeycloakSession session, X509Certificate[] certs, X500Principal crlIssuerPrincipal) throws GeneralSecurityException {

        ......
        X509Certificate currentCRLAnchorCertificate = crlSignatureCertificate;
        X500Principal currentCRLAnchorPrincipal = crlIssuerPrincipal;
        long loop = 1;
        while (true) {
        	log.debugf("Loop nr. '%s' / Checking trust anchor of the CRL issuer '%s' in the CA chain. Anchor is '%s'", loop, certificateCAPrincipals, currentCRLAnchorPrincipal);
        	loop++;
            if (certificateCAPrincipals.contains(currentCRLAnchorPrincipal)) {
                log.tracef("Found trust anchor of the CRL issuer '%s' in the CA chain. Anchor is '%s'", crlIssuerPrincipal, currentCRLAnchorPrincipal);
                break;
            }
        ......
            if (loop > 10000) {
            	throw new GeneralSecurityException("Infinite loop detected, break out!");
            }
        }

        return crlSignatureCertificate;
    }
```

Starting the X.509 login I see 10000 log lines repeating the same check and my `throw new GeneralSecurityException("Infinite loop detected, break out!");`

keycloak logfile:
```
2023-06-01 14:16:48,511 DEBUG [org.keycloak.services:368] [executor-thread-0] Loading CRL from /opt/keycloak-21.1.1/bin/../conf/crl_ca_1.pem
2023-06-01 14:16:48,514 DEBUG [org.keycloak.services:368] [executor-thread-0] Loading CRL from /opt/keycloak-21.1.1/bin/../conf/crl_ca_2.pem
2023-06-01 14:16:48,517 DEBUG [org.keycloak.utils.CRLUtils:123] [executor-thread-0] Loop nr. '1' / Checking trust anchor of the CRL issuer '[OID.2.5.4.97=abcd, CN=xxxxxxx]' in the CA chain. Anchor is 'CN=yyyyyy'

.....

2023-06-01 14:16:48,723 DEBUG [org.keycloak.utils.CRLUtils:123] [executor-thread-0] Loop nr. '9999' / Checking trust anchor of the CRL issuer '[OID.2.5.4.97=abcd, CN=xxxxxxx]' in the CA chain. Anchor is 'CN=yyyyyy'
2023-06-01 14:16:48,723 DEBUG [org.keycloak.utils.CRLUtils:123] [executor-thread-0] Loop nr. '10000' / Checking trust anchor of the CRL issuer '[OID.2.5.4.97=abcd, CN=xxxxxxx]' in the CA chain. Anchor is 'CN=yyyyyy'
2023-06-01 14:16:48,723 ERROR [org.keycloak.services:94] [executor-thread-0] Infinite loop detected, break out!: java.security.GeneralSecurityException: Infinite loop detected, break out!
        at org.keycloak.utils.CRLUtils.findCRLSignatureCertificateInTruststore(CRLUtils.java:142)
        at org.keycloak.utils.CRLUtils.check(CRLUtils.java:75)
        at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.x509.CertificateValidator.checkRevocationStatusUsingCRL(CertificateValidator.java:737)
        at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.x509.CertificateValidator.checkRevocationStatus(CertificateValidator.java:770)
        at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.x509.X509ClientCertificateAuthenticator.authenticate(X509ClientCertificateAuthenticator.java:87)
```



### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Multiple CRL lists are checked correctly against the certificate in use with no infinite loop

### Actual behavior

infinite loop with CPU usage 100%

### How to Reproduce?

Enable and configure more that one CRL list and have a truststore containing both signing CAs

### Anything else?

My issue is not 100% related to #19918 because in the other issue keycloak complains about missing truststore,
which I catched at the beginning too.
But was fixed by usage of `spi-truststore-file-file` instead of `https-trust-store-file`

It looks like in discussion #20468 the same infinite loop was triggered.

Fianally the result of discussion #12310 and it's PR #14961 is not clear,
because to have a working truststore for CRL check, the `spi-truststore-file-file` needs to be used!!!!!!
But in discussion and PR it's stated as configuration for outgoing HTTPS connections.

issue_title: Keycloak admin cli creating/updating authention executions not respecting the priority value specified
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/cli

### Describe the bug

When using the Keycloak admin-cli to create/update authentication flows, it appears that the `priority` value is not being honored. When running `kcadm.sh update authentication/flows/{authenticationFlowID}/executions -f executionConfig.json` (where executionConfig.json is a json file that contains the properties of the execution, including its priority), the other properties of the authentication execution do get properly updated, but the `priority` value does not get updated.

### Version

19.0.3

### Expected behavior

Running `kcadm.sh update authentication/flows/{authenticationFlowID}/executions -f executionConfig.json` for different executions under the same parent flow will update the executions and order them properly based on the priority specified in the json file.

### Actual behavior

Running `kcadm.sh update authentication/flows/{authenticationFlowID}/executions -f executionConfig.json` for different executions under the same parent flow keeps the priority in the order in which they were originally created.

### How to Reproduce?

Create an authentication flow with 2 authenticationExecutions as its children.

Create configuration json files for each of the executions with "priority": 1 for the authenticationExecution that was created first and  "priority": 0 for the authenticationExecution that was created second.

Run `kcadm.sh update authentication/flows/{authenticationFlowID}/executions -f executionConfig.json` for both execution config files.

Verify the order of the executions using the Keycloak admin console.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Token-exchange is not working for OpenID Connect v1.0 provider in KC 21.1.1
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

In our project we make use of the token-exchange feature and heavily rely on this implementation. We are running version 20.0.5 where the token-exchange feature for OpenID Connect v1.0 providers works flawlessly. However when we updated to the latest version the token-exchange feature does not work how we expected it should work.

In version 20.0.5 we see our custom claims map with the token-exchange. Now we get only the default claims (and no custom claims).

I've started from scratch to determine if it is in our data or that it is an actual bug in keycloak. To confirm, it is a possible bug in keycloak because from scratch it didn't work either.

I've also tested the nightly build, the problem exists there as well.

During our security tests we discovered that there was a bug in 20.0.5 and that we should upgrade to version 21.0.0 where the specific security threat was solved. That's the main reason we wanted to upgrade/upgrade to the latest version.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

That the custom claims defined in scopes are returned when the token-exchange has occurred (i believe this is called internal-to-internal token-exchange).

### Actual behavior

The custom claims defined are not displayed/mapped in the token-exchange token. For example:

Example (this is token I use to exchange):

`{
  "exp": 1686083423,
  "iat": 1686083123,
  "jti": "158952f9-003a-4263-b837-e74797ea391d",

  "acr": "1",
  "allowed-origins": [
    "/*"
  ],
  "realm_access": {
    "roles": [
      "default-roles-myrealm",
      "offline_access",
      "uma_authorization"
    ]
  },
  "resource_access": {
    "account": {
      "roles": [
        "manage-account",

        "view-profile"
      ]
    }
  },
  "scope": "openid email custom-customer-realm profile",
  "customer_realm": "myrealm",
  "email_verified": false,
  "clientHost": "172.19.0.1",
  "preferred_username": "service-account-myclient-test",
  "clientAddress": "172.19.0.1",
  "client_id": "myclient-test"
}`

After exchange:

`{
  "exp": 1686083505,
  "iat": 1686083205,
  "jti": "cec6821f-49c2-4e93-b090-ce05cc0bf0ac",
  "iss": "http://localhost:8080/realms/mymainrealm",
  "aud": [
    "account",
    "mymainrealm-api"
  ],
  "sub": "387e9458-f10b-4477-8eca-2e05c9f866d2",
  "typ": "Bearer",
  "azp": "mymainrealm-myclient",
  "session_state": "bc1a090e-3247-47e1-8287-48f6ec472837",
  "acr": "1",
  "allowed-origins": [
    "/*"
  ],
  "realm_access": {
    "roles": [
      "offline_access",
      "uma_authorization",
      "default-roles-mymainrealm"
    ]
  },
  "resource_access": {
    "account": {
      "roles": [
        "manage-account",
        "manage-account-links",
        "view-profile"
      ]
    }
  },
  "scope": "mymainrealm-customer-realm email profile",
  "sid": "bc1a090e-3247-47e1-8287-48f6ec472837",
  "email_verified": false,
  "preferred_username": "service-account-myclient-test"
}`

As you can see the customer_realm custom claim is not displayed/mapped after the token-exchange. In version 20.0.5 it is mapped.

### How to Reproduce?

Start from scratch in version 21.1.1 (I've tested it in version 21.0.0 and the problem we face was already introduced in this version). The following features are enabled:

- token-exchange
- admin-fine-grained-authz

1. Add two realms: mymainrealm and myrealm
2. Navigate to myrealm
3. Create a client-scope with the following settings:
name: custom-customer-realm
type: Default
protocol: openid connect
display on consent: yes
include in token scope: yes
All other fields, leave them with the default value

4. Create the following mapper in the recently created client-scope (custom-customer-realm) with the following settings:
mapper type: hardcoded claim
name: customer-realm-mapper
token claim name: customer_realm
claim value: myrealm
claim json type: string
id token: yes
access token: yes
userinfo: yes
All other fields, leave them with the default value

5. Create the following client:
client id: myclient-test
client authentication: yes
authentication flow(s): standard flow, service account roles
All other fields, leave them with the default value

6. Enable the permissions for the just created client (myclient-test)
7. Create a token exchange policy for the just created client and attach this token-exchange policy to the just created client
8. Create the following client:
client id: mymainclient
client authentication: yes
authentication flow(s): standard flow, direct access grants
All other fields, leave them with the default value

9. Get the client secret of the just created client (mymainclient), write this down
10. Navigate to the realm settings to the endpoints section. Click on OpenID Endpoint Configuration and write the url down
11. Navigate to mymainrealm
12. Create a client-scope with the following setting:
name: mymainrealm-customer-realm
type: default
protocol: OpenID connect
consent screen: yes
token scope: yes
All other fields, leave them with the default value

13. Create the following mapper in the recently created client-scope (mymainrealm-customer-realm) with the following settings:
mapper type: user attribute
name: mymainrealm-customer-realm
user attribute: customer_realm
token claim name: customer_realm
claim json type: string
id token: yes
access token: yes
userinfo: yes
All other fields, leave them with the default value

14. Create the following client:
client id: mymainrealm-myclient
All other fields, leave them with the default value

15. Create the following client:
client id: mymainrealm-api
client authentication: yes
authentication flow(s): standard flow, direct access grants, service account roles
All other fields, leave them with the default value

16. Enable the permissions for the just created client (mymainrealm-api)
17. Create a token exchange policy pointing to the client (mymainrealm-myclient) and attach this token-exchange policy to the just created client (mymainrealm-api)
18. Create a new OpenID Connect v1.0 Identity provider (you are still in the mymainrealm realm) with the following settings:
alias: somedomain.com
discovery endpoint: the endpoint you have written down in step 10
client id: mymainclient
client secret: the client secret you have written down in step 9
All other fields, leave them with the default value

19. Create the following mapper in the recently created identity provider (somedomain.com) with the following settings:
name: custom-customer-realm-mapper
sync mode: force
mapper type: attribute importer
claim: customer_realm
user attribute: customer_realm
All other fields, leave them with the default value

20. Enable the permissions for the just created identity provider (somedomain.com)
21. Create a token exchange policy pointing to the client (mymainrealm-myclient) and attach this token-exchange policy to the just created identity provider (somedomain.com)

If all these steps are followed correctly, you need to execute the following requests with the help of Postman:

First request:
POST: http://localhost:8080/realms/myrealm/protocol/openid-connect/token
grant_type: client_credentials
client_id: myclient-test
client_secret: the secret which you can get from the myrealm client
scope: openid

If you verify the token which is returned from the request you can see the customer_realm custom claim

Execute the following request with the help of Postman:
POST: http://localhost:8080/realms/mymainrealm/protocol/openid-connect/token
grant_type: urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange
client_id: mymainrealm-myclient
subject_token: the access token that is returned from the first request
subject_issuer: http://localhost:8080/realms/myrealm
audiance: mymainrealm-api

If you verify the token which is returned after the token-exchange you can see that the customer_realm custom claim does not exist anymore (and in version 20.0.5 it works).

Sorry for the long list of steps to execute ;-) ...

### Anything else?

What I've seen from the other responses, you guys seems to be busy. Hopefully you get some time to review the bug and confirm if it is a bug or not. Otherwise we need to stay on version 20.0.5 until this is resolved with a potential security risk.

issue_title: Webauthn signature algorithms are improperly encoded as strings
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have searched existing issues - [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release ### Area authentication/webauthn ### Describe the bug With some third-party webauthn authenticators, in this case 1Password's browser beta browser extension, Keycloak will fail to register a passwordless credential during setup. The root cause of this the [use](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/ecf4dbfb18edf01d1f8dd6682b97371540447fa8/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/requiredactions/WebAuthnRegister.java#LL120C1-L120C1) of [stringified integers](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/989ffe2d228374847565c34f6ffd14afcfb31337/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/webauthn-register.ftl#LL39C23-L39C23) to describe the requested public key algorithm in the `navigator.credentials.create()` `publicKey` parameters. For example, this is what a registration looks like with the current version of Keycloak. Notice how the `pubKeyCredParams` `alg` fields are stringified: The string typing, while accepted by all the large platform authenticators (Android, Apple, Google, and Windows) who try to be lenient for compatibility, is technically going against the spec. Both [MDN's documentation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CredentialsContainer/create#publickey_object_structure) and the [webauthn spec](https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn-2/#sctn-alg-identifier) document it to be an integer. [Other](https://github.com/1Password/passkey-rs/blob/4561e8f5fc5ac21177f3505c5961dd0ba4196bc2/passkey-types/src/webauthn/attestation.rs#L249-L258) [libraries](https://github.com/kanidm/webauthn-rs/blob/2db3a1a873c8d316e4782c80d8dc4f5bd6d86233/webauthn-rs-proto/src/options.rs#L105-L106) have also followed suit. As a result, the credential setup request generated by Keycloak may fail to validate depending on the library being used to represent types. ### Version 21.1.1 ### Expected behavior Keycloak's webauthn credential creation requests would follow the standards and describe public key algorithms are integers in JavaScript, therefore being accepted by all authenticators, even if they follow the specification tightly. ### Actual behavior Keycloak encodes webauthn public key algorithms as stringified integers, resulting in validation and deserialization failures when using stricter webauthn client libraries on the produced requests/data. ### How to Reproduce? 1. Setup a functional Keycloak instance 2. Configure a new realm 3. Create an authentication flow that includes a `WebAuthn Passwordless Authenticator` step somewhere. 4. Bind this authentication flow to the browser flow. 5. Create a basic account with a pre-assigned password. 6. Go to `http://localhost:8080/realms/realm_name/account/#/` and sign-in using that account. 7. Navigate to `Signing in` and scroll to the bottom 8. Click `Set up Security Key` Once you are on the registration page, open the browser inspector and find the `login-actions/required-action` page generated for this session. Once its been located, inspect the HTML and find the the line starting with `let signatureAlgorithms =`. Observe how the algorithm(s) are encoded as strings instead of integers: ``` let signatureAlgorithms = "-7"; let pubKeyCredParams = getPubKeyCredParams(signatureAlgorithms); ``` ### Anything else? _No response_
commit_message:
Pass webauthn signature algorithm IDs as integers instead of strings (#20832)

closes #20831

issue_title: [Declarative User Profile] Attributes tab doesn't dipslay attributes, values get lost and multi values get stored as single ones
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

User attributes are no longer shown in the attributes tab.
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/789496/4aa35f24-081d-4e85-aff8-fae7575dd4d1)

They are only shown in the User details tab.
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/789496/bc3cdab4-131c-4ed3-93d3-00fc004ab1b3)

## Bugs

* When adding a user attribute at Attribute tab, other existing stored attribute values get lost
* When editing a multi value user attribute at the user details tab (e.g. modify "A,B" to "A,B,C", it is stored as single comma separated value "A,B,C")



### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

- No data gets lost when adding attribute values
- Multi value attributes are not stored as single value when edited
- Attributes tab displays all stored attributes 

### Actual behavior

see section "Describe the bug" 

### How to Reproduce?

1. Define two attributes in the declarative user profile (in the screenshots above "tel" and "orgs" is defined).
2. Add "tel: 1111", "orgs: A" and "orgs:B" in the attributes tab
3. Modify "orgs: A,B" to "orgs: A,B,C" in user details tab will store it as "A,B,C"
4. Add attribute "tel: 1234" in attributes tab will delete the orgs value


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Empty shortVerificationUri not the same with default (null) value
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Can't set a default value (null) for "Short verification_uri in Device" after save

Related with #16107

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/grants/device/endpoints/DeviceEndpoint.java#L173

### Version

21.1

### Expected behavior

The same behavior for null and empty value

### Actual behavior

When I set empty shortVerificationUri
Got **verification_uri_complete** like "?user_code=WKAK-VPGW" instead "https://host.com/realms/<realm>/device?user_code=WKAK-VPGW"

### How to Reproduce?

- add device flow authentification to the client
- try to get verification_uri from {{KEYCLOAK_URL}}/realms/{{KEYCLOAK_REALM}}/protocol/openid-connect/auth/device and get the correct URL (device flow)
- go to https://<host>/admin/master/console/#/<realm>/realm-settings/tokens
- save settings with empty "Short verification_uri in Device" 
- call {{KEYCLOAK_URL}}/realms/{{KEYCLOAK_REALM}}/protocol/openid-connect/auth/device (device flow) and get like "?user_code=WKAK-VPGW" not the correct URL


### Anything else?

For fix you can go to DB and remove the record 
WHERE name = 'shortVerificationUri' and realm_id='<id>'

Empty shortVerificationUri not the same with default (null) value
